QuartzRestApi
Table of Contents

Class SchedulerController

Namespace
QuartzRestApi.Controllers
Assembly
QuartzRestApi.dll

Provides endpoints for managing and monitoring a Quartz.NET IScheduler instance. This controller exposes various operations such as checking the status of job and trigger groups, retrieving scheduler metadata, and managing jobs and triggers.

[ApiController]
public class SchedulerController : ControllerBase
Inheritance
object

Constructors

SchedulerController(IScheduler, ILogger)

Makes a new instance of the SchedulerController class.

public SchedulerController(IScheduler scheduler, ILogger logger = null)

Parameters

scheduler IScheduler

Quartz.IScheduler

logger ILogger

ILogger

Methods

AddCalendar(string)

Add (register) the given Quartz.ICalendar to the Scheduler.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/AddCalendar")]
public Task AddCalendar(string json)

Parameters

json string

The Quartz.ICalendar information

Returns

Task

AddJob(string)

Add the given Quartz.IJob to the Scheduler - with no associated Quartz.ITrigger. The Quartz.IJob will be 'dormant' until it is scheduled with a Quartz.ITrigger, or TriggerJob /> is called for it.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/AddJob")]
public Task AddJob(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

With the StoreNonDurableWhileAwaitingScheduling parameter set to 

true
, a non-durable job can be stored. Once it is scheduled, it will resume normal non-durable behavior (i.e. be deleted once there are no remaining associated triggers).

CheckExistsJobKey(string)

Determine whether a Quartz.IJob with the given identifier already exists within the scheduler.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/checkexistsjobkey")]
public bool CheckExistsJobKey(string json)

Parameters

json string

the identifier to check for

Returns

bool

true if a Job exists with the given identifier

CheckExistsTriggerKey(string)

Determine whether a Quartz.ITrigger with the given identifier already exists within the scheduler.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/checkexiststriggerkey")]
public bool CheckExistsTriggerKey(string json)

Parameters

json string

the identifier to check for

Returns

bool

true if a Trigger exists with the given identifier

Clear()

Clears (deletes!) all scheduling data - all Quartz.IJobs, Quartz.ITriggers Quartz.ICalendars.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/Clear")]
public void Clear()

Context()

Returns the Quartz.SchedulerContext of the Quartz.IScheduler.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/SchedulerContext")]
public string Context()

Returns

string

DeleteCalendar(string)

Delete the identified Quartz.ICalendar from the Scheduler.

[HttpDelete]
[Route("Scheduler/DeleteCalendar/{calName}")]
public bool DeleteCalendar(string calName)

Parameters

calName string

Name of the calendar.

Returns

bool

true if the Calendar was found and deleted.

Remarks

If removal of the 

Calendar
would result in Quartz.ITriggers pointing to non-existent calendars, then a Quartz.SchedulerException will be thrown.

DeleteJob(string)

Delete the identified Quartz.IJob from the Scheduler - and any associated Quartz.ITriggers.

[HttpDelete]
[Route("Scheduler/DeleteJob")]
public Task<bool> DeleteJob(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task<bool>

true if the Job was found and deleted.

DeleteJobs(string)

Delete the identified jobs from the Scheduler - and any associated Quartz.ITriggers.

[HttpDelete]
[Route("Scheduler/DeleteJobs")]
public Task<bool> DeleteJobs(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task<bool>

true if all the Jobs were found and deleted, false if one or more were not deleted.

Remarks

Note that while this bulk operation is likely more efficient than invoking DeleteJob(string) several times, it may have the adverse affect of holding data locks for a single long duration of time (rather than lots of small durations of time).

GetCalendar(string)

Get the Quartz.ICalendar instance with the given name.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/Getcalendar/{calName}")]
public string GetCalendar(string calName)

Parameters

calName string

Returns

string

GetCalendarNames()

Get the names of all registered Quartz.ICalendar.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetCalendarNames")]
public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetCalendarNames()

Returns

Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>>

GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs()

Return a list of Quartz.IJobExecutionContext objects that represent all currently executing Jobs in this Scheduler instance.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs")]
public string GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs()

Returns

string

Remarks

This method is not cluster aware. That is, it will only return Jobs currently executing in this Scheduler instance, not across the entire cluster.

Note that the list returned is an 'instantaneous' snapshot, and that as soon as it's returned, the true list of executing jobs may be different. Also, please read the doc associated with Quartz.IJobExecutionContext- especially if you're using remoting.

See Also
IJobExecutionContext

GetJobDetail(string)

Get the Quartz.IJobDetail for the Quartz.IJob instance with the given key.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetJobDetail")]
public string GetJobDetail(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

string

Remarks

The returned JobDetail object will be a snapshot of the actual stored JobDetail. If you wish to modify the JobDetail, you must re-store the JobDetail afterward (e.g. see AddJob(string)).

GetJobGroupNames()

Get the names of all known Quartz.IJobDetail groups.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetJobGroupNames")]
public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetJobGroupNames()

Returns

Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>>

GetJobKeys(string)

Get the keys of all the Quartz.IJobDetails in the matching groups.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetJobKeys")]
public string GetJobKeys(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

string

GetMetaData()

Get a Quartz.SchedulerMetaData object describing the settings and capabilities of the scheduler instance.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetMetaData")]
public string GetMetaData()

Returns

string

Remarks

Note that the data returned is an 'instantaneous' snapshot, and that as soon as it's returned, the meta-data values may be different.

GetPausedTriggerGroups()

Get the names of all Quartz.ITrigger groups that are paused.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetPausedTriggerGroups")]
public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetPausedTriggerGroups()

Returns

Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>>

GetTrigger(string)

Get the Quartz.ITrigger instance with the given key.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetTrigger")]
public string GetTrigger(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

string

Remarks

The returned Trigger object will be a snapshot of the actual stored trigger. If you wish to modify the trigger, you must re-store the trigger afterward (e.g. see RescheduleJob(string)).

GetTriggerGroupNames()

Get the names of all known Quartz.ITrigger groups.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetTriggerGroupNames")]
public Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>> GetTriggerGroupNames()

Returns

Task<IReadOnlyCollection<string>>

GetTriggerKeys(string)

Get the names of all the Quartz.ITriggers in the given groups.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetTriggerKeys")]
public string GetTriggerKeys(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

string

GetTriggerState(string)

Get the current state of the identified Quartz.ITrigger.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetTriggerState")]
public string GetTriggerState(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

string
See Also
Normal
Paused
Complete
Blocked
Error
None

GetTriggersOfJob(string)

Get all Quartz.ITrigger s that are associated with the identified Quartz.IJobDetail.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/GetTriggersOfJob")]
public string GetTriggersOfJob(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

string

Remarks

The returned Trigger objects will be snapshots of the actual stored triggers. If you wish to modify a trigger, you must re-store the trigger afterward (e.g. see RescheduleJob(string)).

InStandbyMode()

Reports whether the Quartz.IScheduler is in stand-by mode.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/InStandbyMode")]
public bool InStandbyMode()

Returns

bool
See Also
Standby()
Start()

InterruptFireInstanceId(string)

Request the cancellation, within this Scheduler instance, of the identified executing job instance.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/interruptfireinstanceid/{fireInstanceId}")]
public bool InterruptFireInstanceId(string fireInstanceId)

Parameters

fireInstanceId string

the unique identifier of the job instance to be interrupted (see Quartz.IJobExecutionContext.FireInstanceId)

Returns

bool

true if the identified job instance was found and interrupted.

Remarks

This method is not cluster aware. That is, it will only interrupt instances of the identified InterruptableJob currently executing in this Scheduler instance, not across the entire cluster.

See Also
GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs()
FireInstanceId
InterruptJobKey(string)

InterruptJobKey(string)

Request the cancellation, within this Scheduler instance, of all currently executing instances of the identified Quartz.IJob.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/InterruptJobKey")]
public bool InterruptJobKey(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

bool

true is at least one instance of the identified job was found and interrupted.

Remarks

If more than one instance of the identified job is currently executing, the cancellation token will be set on each instance. However, there is a limitation that in the case that InterruptJobKey(string) on one instances throws an exception, all remaining instances (that have not yet been interrupted) will not have their InterruptJobKey(string) method called.

If you wish to interrupt a specific instance of a job (when more than one is executing) you can do so by calling GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs() to obtain a handle to the job instance, and then invoke InterruptJobKey(string) on it yourself.

This method is not cluster aware. That is, it will only interrupt instances of the identified InterruptableJob currently executing in this Scheduler instance, not across the entire cluster.

See Also
GetCurrentlyExecutingJobs()

IsJobGroupPaused(string)

Returns true if the given JobGroup is paused

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/IsJobGroupPaused/{groupName}")]
public Task<bool> IsJobGroupPaused(string groupName)

Parameters

groupName string

The group name

Returns

Task<bool>

IsShutdown()

Reports whether the Quartz.IScheduler has been Shutdown.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/Isshutdown")]
public bool IsShutdown()

Returns

bool

IsStarted()

Whether the scheduler has been started.

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/IsStarted")]
public bool IsStarted()

Returns

bool

Remarks

Note: This only reflects whether Start() has ever been called on this Scheduler, so it will return true even if the Quartz.IScheduler is currently in standby mode or has been since shutdown.

See Also
Start()
IsShutdown()
InStandbyMode()

IsTriggerGroupPaused(string)

Returns true if the given TriggerGroup is paused

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/IsTriggerGroupPaused/{groupName}")]
public Task<bool> IsTriggerGroupPaused(string groupName)

Parameters

groupName string

The group name

Returns

Task<bool>

PauseAll()

Pause all triggers - similar to calling PauseTriggers(string) on every group, however, after using this method ResumeAll() must be called to clear the scheduler's state of 'remembering' that all new triggers will be paused as they are added.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/PauseAllTriggers")]
public void PauseAll()

Remarks

When ResumeAll() is called (to un-pause), trigger misfire instructions WILL be applied.

See Also
ResumeAll()
PauseTriggers(string)
Standby()

PauseJob(string)

Pause the Quartz.IJobDetail with the given key - by pausing all of its current Quartz.ITriggers.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/PauseJob")]
public Task PauseJob(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

PauseJobs(string)

Pause all the Quartz.IJobDetails in the matching groups - by pausing all of their Quartz.ITriggers.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/PauseJobs")]
public Task PauseJobs(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

The Scheduler will "remember" that the groups are paused, and impose the pause on any new jobs that are added to any of those groups until it is resumed.

NOTE: There is a limitation that only exactly matched groups can be remembered as paused. For example, if there are pre-existing job in groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause groups that start with "a" then the group "aaa" will be remembered as paused and any subsequently added jobs in group "aaa" will be paused, however if a job is added to group "axx" it will not be paused, as "axx" wasn't known at the time the "group starts with a" matcher was applied. HOWEVER, if there are pre-existing groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause the group "axx" (with a group equals matcher) then no jobs will be paused, but it will be remembered that group "axx" is paused and later when a job is added in that group, it will become paused.

See Also
ResumeJobs(string)

PauseTrigger(string)

Pause the Quartz.ITrigger with the given key.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/PauseTrigger")]
public Task PauseTrigger(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

PauseTriggers(string)

Pause all the Quartz.ITriggers in the groups matching.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/PauseTriggers")]
public Task PauseTriggers(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

The Scheduler will "remember" all the groups paused, and impose the pause on any new triggers that are added to any of those groups until it is resumed.

NOTE: There is a limitation that only exactly matched groups can be remembered as paused. For example, if there are pre-existing triggers in groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause groups that start with "a" then the group "aaa" will be remembered as paused and any subsequently added triggers in that group be paused, however if a trigger is added to group "axx" it will not be paused, as "axx" wasn't known at the time the "group starts with a" matcher was applied. HOWEVER, if there are pre-existing groups "aaa" and "bbb" and a matcher is given to pause the group "axx" (with a group equals matcher) then no triggers will be paused, but it will be remembered that group "axx" is paused and later when a trigger is added in that group, it will become paused.

See Also
ResumeTriggers(string)

RescheduleJob(string)

Remove (delete) the Quartz.ITrigger with the given key, and store the new given one - which must be associated with the same job (the new trigger must have the job name & group specified) - however, the new trigger need not have the same name as the old trigger.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/RescheduleJob")]
public Task<DateTimeOffset?> RescheduleJob(string json)

Parameters

json string

The json

Returns

Task<DateTimeOffset?>

null if a Quartz.ITrigger with the given name and group was not found and removed from the store (and the new trigger is therefore not stored), otherwise the first fire time of the newly scheduled trigger.

ResetTriggerFromErrorState(string)

Remove the given Quartz.ITrigger from the error state and set it back to Quartz.TriggerState.Normal or Quartz.TriggerState.Paused as appropriate.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ResetTriggerFromErrorState")]
public Task ResetTriggerFromErrorState(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

Only affects triggers that are in Quartz.TriggerState.Error state. If the trigger is not in that state, the call is a no-op.

ResumeAll()

Resume (un-pause) all triggers - similar to calling ResumeTriggers(string) on every group.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ResumeAllTriggers")]
public void ResumeAll()

Remarks

If any Quartz.ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied.

See Also
PauseAll()

ResumeJob(string)

Resume (un-pause) the Quartz.IJobDetail with the given key.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ResumeJob")]
public Task ResumeJob(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

If any of the Quartz.IJob'sQuartz.ITrigger s missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied.

ResumeJobs(string)

Resume (un-pause) all the Quartz.IJobDetails in matching groups.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ResumeJobs")]
public Task ResumeJobs(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

If any of the Quartz.IJob s had Quartz.ITrigger s that missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied.

See Also
PauseJobs(string)

ResumeTrigger(string)

Resume (un-pause) the Quartz.ITrigger with the given key.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ResumeTrigger")]
public Task ResumeTrigger(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

If the Quartz.ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied.

ResumeTriggers(string)

Resume (un-pause) all the Quartz.ITriggers in matching groups.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ResumeTriggers")]
public Task ResumeTriggers(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

If any Quartz.ITrigger missed one or more fire-times, then the Quartz.ITrigger's misfire instruction will be applied.

See Also
PauseTriggers(string)

ScheduleJob(string)

Add the given Quartz.IJobDetail to the Scheduler, and associate the given Quartz.ITrigger with it.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ScheduleJobWithJobDetailAndTrigger")]
public Task<DateTimeOffset> ScheduleJob(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task<DateTimeOffset>

Remarks

If the given Trigger does not reference any Quartz.IJob, then it will be set to reference the Job passed with it into this method.

ScheduleJobIdentifiedWithTrigger(string)

Schedule the given Quartz.ITrigger with the Quartz.IJob identified by the Quartz.ITrigger's settings.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ScheduleJobIdentifiedWithTrigger")]
public Task<DateTimeOffset> ScheduleJobIdentifiedWithTrigger(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task<DateTimeOffset>

ScheduleJobWithTriggers(string)

Schedule the given job with the related set of triggers.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ScheduleJobWithJobDetailAndTriggers")]
public Task ScheduleJobWithTriggers(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

If any of the given job or triggers already exist (or more specifically, if the keys are not unique) and the replace parameter is not set to true then an exception will be thrown.

ScheduleJobs(string)

Schedule all the given jobs with the related set of triggers.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/ScheduleJobs")]
public Task ScheduleJobs(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

Remarks

If any of the given jobs or triggers already exist (or more specifically, if the keys are not unique) and the replace parameter is not set to true then an exception will be thrown.

SchedulerInstanceId()

Returns the instance id of the scheduler

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/SchedulerInstanceId")]
public string SchedulerInstanceId()

Returns

string

SchedulerName()

Returns the name of the scheduler

[HttpGet]
[Route("Scheduler/SchedulerName")]
public string SchedulerName()

Returns

string

Shutdown()

Halts the Quartz.IScheduler's firing of Quartz.ITriggers, and cleans up all resources associated with the Scheduler. Equivalent to Shutdown(bool).

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/Shutdown")]
public Task Shutdown()

Returns

Task

Remarks

The scheduler cannot be re-started.

See Also
Shutdown(bool)

Shutdown(bool)

Halts the Quartz.IScheduler's firing of Quartz.ITriggers, and cleans up all resources associated with the Scheduler.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/Shutdown/{waitForJobsToComplete}")]
public Task Shutdown(bool waitForJobsToComplete)

Parameters

waitForJobsToComplete bool

if true the scheduler will not allow this method to return until all currently executing jobs have completed.

Returns

Task

Remarks

The scheduler cannot be re-started.

See Also
Shutdown()

Standby()

Temporarily halts the Quartz.IScheduler's firing of Quartz.ITriggers.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/Standby")]
public Task Standby()

Returns

Task

Remarks

When Start() is called (to bring the scheduler out of stand-by mode), trigger misfire instructions will NOT be applied during the execution of the Start() method - any misfires will be detected immediately afterward (by the Quartz.Spi.IJobStore's normal process).

The scheduler is not destroyed, and can be re-started at any time.

See Also
Start()
PauseAll()

Start()

Starts the Quartz.IScheduler's threads that fire Quartz.ITriggers. When a scheduler is first created it is in "stand-by" mode, and will not fire triggers. The scheduler can also be put into stand-by mode by calling the Standby() method.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/Start")]
public Task Start()

Returns

Task

Remarks

The misfire/recovery process will be started, if it is the initial call to this method on this scheduler instance.

See Also
StartDelayed(int)
Standby()
Shutdown(bool)

StartDelayed(int)

Calls Start() after the indicated delay. (This call does not block). This can be useful within applications that have initializers that create the scheduler immediately, before the resources needed by the executing jobs have been fully initialized.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/StartDelayed/{delay}")]
public Task StartDelayed(int delay)

Parameters

delay int

Returns

Task
See Also
Start()
Standby()
Shutdown(bool)

TriggerJobWithDataMap(string)

Trigger the identified Quartz.IJobDetail (Execute it now).

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/TriggerJobWithDataMap")]
public Task TriggerJobWithDataMap(string json)

Parameters

json string

The JobKey with associated Quartz.JobDataMap of the Quartz.IJob to be executed.

Returns

Task

TriggerJobWithJobKey(string)

Trigger the identified Quartz.IJobDetail (Execute it now).

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/TriggerJobWithJobkey")]
public Task TriggerJobWithJobKey(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task

UnscheduleJob(string)

Remove the indicated Quartz.ITrigger from the scheduler.

If the related job does not have any other triggers, and the job is not durable, then the job will also be deleted. 

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/UnscheduleJob")]
public Task<bool> UnscheduleJob(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task<bool>

UnscheduleJobs(string)

Remove all the indicated Quartz.ITriggers from the scheduler.

[HttpPost]
[Route("Scheduler/UnscheduleJobs")]
public Task<bool> UnscheduleJobs(string json)

Parameters

json string

Returns

Task<bool>

Remarks

If the related job does not have any other triggers, and the job is not durable, then the job will also be deleted.

Note that while this bulk operation is likely more efficient than invoking UnscheduleJob(string) several times, it may have the adverse affect of holding data locks for a single long duration of time (rather than lots of small durations of time).
